req.title: Manager updates team
req.statement: An authenticated manager shall be able to update a team through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers an authenticated manager updating a team via a UI form. Four distinct test cases are designed: (1) EP-positive: valid full update with all mandatory fields supplied, verifying 200/201, entity id, and audit record; (2) NEG-negative: submission with a blank mandatory field, expecting a 400-equivalent validation error visible in the UI; (3) BVA-boundary: team name at the maximum allowed length (255 chars), probing the exact upper edge; (4) DT-negative: unauthenticated user attempt, expecting a 401/redirect to login. Each test is self-contained via fixtures that create and then clean up test data, making them idempotent.

tests.name: test_manager_updates_team_with_valid_data
preconditions: User is authenticated as a manager
A pre-existing team record exists with known id
actions: Open the edit form for the target team
Fill all mandatory fields with valid values
expected: Response body contains the updated entity id
Team record reflects new values in the UI
An audit record is written for the update action
trace: R-WEB-010: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_manager_updates_team_with_valid_data(
    manager_page,        # <fixture>: authenticated Playwright Page for manager role
    existing_team,       # <fixture>: creates team, yields {id, name}, deletes after test
    audit_log_checker,   # <fixture>: helper to query audit records
):
    page = manager_page
    team_id = existing_team["id"]
    new_name = "Updated Team Alpha"

    page.goto("/teams")
    page.get_by_test_id(f"edit-team-{team_id}").click()

    page.get_by_label("Team Name").fill(new_name)
    page.get_by_label("Department").fill("Engineering")

    with page.expect_response(
        lambda r: "/teams" in r.url and r.request.method in ("POST", "PUT")
    ) as resp_info:
        page.get_by_role("button", name="Save").click()

    response = resp_info.value
    assert response.status in (200, 201), f"Expected 200/201, got {response.status}"

    body = response.json()
    assert "id" in body, "Response must contain entity id"

    expect(page.get_by_test_id(f"team-name-{team_id}")).to_have_text(new_name)

    assert audit_log_checker.has_record(
        entity="team", entity_id=team_id, action="update"
    ), "Audit record not found for team update"

tests.name: test_manager_update_team_fails_on_blank_mandatory_field
preconditions: User is authenticated as a manager
A pre-existing team record exists with known id
actions: Open the edit form for the target team
Clear the mandatory Team Name field
expected: Form submission is rejected (HTTP 400 or client-side validation)
Validation error message is visible on the page
Team record is not updated in the backend
trace: R-WEB-010: mandatory fields must be validated
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_manager_update_team_fails_on_blank_mandatory_field(
    manager_page,
    existing_team,
    audit_log_checker,
):
    page = manager_page
    team_id = existing_team["id"]
    original_name = existing_team["name"]

    page.goto("/teams")
    page.get_by_test_id(f"edit-team-{team_id}").click()

    page.get_by_label("Team Name").fill("")

    api_responses = []
    page.on("response", lambda r: api_responses.append(r)
            if "/teams" in r.url else None)

    page.get_by_role("button", name="Save").click()

    error_locator = page.get_by_test_id("field-error-team-name")
    expect(error_locator).to_be_visible()

    api_calls = [r for r in api_responses
                 if r.request.method in ("POST", "PUT")]
    for call in api_calls:
        assert call.status == 400, f"Expected 400, got {call.status}"

    assert not audit_log_checker.has_record(
        entity="team", entity_id=team_id, action="update"
    ), "Audit record must NOT be written on validation failure"

    page.reload()
    expect(page.get_by_test_id(f"team-name-{team_id}")).to_have_text(original_name)

tests.name: test_manager_update_team_name_at_max_boundary_length
preconditions: User is authenticated as a manager
A pre-existing team record exists with known id
Maximum allowed length for Team Name is 255 characters
actions: Open the edit form for the target team
Fill Team Name with exactly 255 characters
expected: Team Name is persisted at exactly 255 characters
trace: R-WEB-010: mandatory fields must be validated
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

MAX_NAME_LEN = 255

def test_manager_update_team_name_at_max_boundary_length(
    manager_page,
    existing_team,
    audit_log_checker,
):
    page = manager_page
    team_id = existing_team["id"]
    boundary_name = "T" * MAX_NAME_LEN
    assert len(boundary_name) == MAX_NAME_LEN

    page.goto("/teams")
    page.get_by_test_id(f"edit-team-{team_id}").click()

    name_input = page.get_by_label("Team Name")
    name_input.fill(boundary_name)
    assert len(name_input.input_value()) == MAX_NAME_LEN, "Input truncated unexpectedly"

    with page.expect_response(
        lambda r: "/teams" in r.url and r.request.method in ("POST", "PUT")
    ) as resp_info:
        page.get_by_role("button", name="Save").click()

    response = resp_info.value
    assert response.status in (200, 201), f"Expected 200/201, got {response.status}"
    body = response.json()
    assert "id" in body

    assert audit_log_checker.has_record(
        entity="team", entity_id=team_id, action="update"
    ), "Audit record missing for max-length name update"

    persisted = <helper_fn>.get_team_by_id(team_id)
    assert len(persisted["name"]) == MAX_NAME_LEN

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_update_team
preconditions: A pre-existing team record exists with known id
actions: Attempt to navigate directly to /teams without a valid session
Attempt a direct PUT API call to /teams/{id} without auth token
expected: UI redirects to login page or shows 401/403 message
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
from playwright.sync_api import expect

BASE_URL = <helper_fn>.get_base_url()   # <helper_fn>: returns app base URL string

def test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_update_team(
    browser_context_no_auth,  # <fixture>: Playwright BrowserContext with cleared cookies
    existing_team,
    audit_log_checker,
):
    team_id = existing_team["id"]
    page = browser_context_no_auth.new_page()

    page.goto("/teams")
    expect(page).to_have_url(lambda url: "login" in url or "signin" in url)

    api_resp = requests.put(
        f"{BASE_URL}/teams/{team_id}",
        json={"name": "Unauthorized Update"},
        headers={"Content-Type": "application/json"},
        timeout=10,
    )
    assert api_resp.status_code == 401, (
        f"Expected 401 for unauthenticated API call, got {api_resp.status_code}"
    )

    assert not audit_log_checker.has_record(
        entity="team", entity_id=team_id, action="update"
    ), "Audit record must NOT be written for unauthenticated attempt"

    page.close()
